LKQ Cuts 2025 Free Cash Flow View to $600M-$750M From $750M-$900M >LKQ
Supply chain firm Ryder's Q2 revenue remains flat
data.description:
Overview

  • Ryder Q2 adjusted EPS rises 11% yr/yr, beating analyst expectations

  • Total revenue for Q2 remains flat at $3.2 bln

  • Operating revenue grows 2% driven by SCS and FMS contractual growth

Outlook

  • Ryder expects FY25 comparable EPS of $12.85 - $13.30

  • Company raises FY25 free cash flow forecast by $500 mln

  • Ryder anticipates 3Q25 comparable EPS of $3.45 - $3.65

  • Company projects FY25 adjusted ROE of 17%

Result Drivers

  • CONTRACTUAL PORTFOLIO - Earnings growth driven by resilient contractual portfolio and strategic initiatives

  • SCS PERFORMANCE - Record earnings in SCS due to strategic initiatives and new business

  • FMS CHALLENGES - Contractual earnings growth in FMS partially offset by weaker used vehicle sales market

Key Details

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy", 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the ground freight & logistics peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ryder System Inc is $183.00, about 5.6% above its July 23 closing price of $172.82

  • The stock recently traded at 13 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 10 three months ago

data.title: Turkey Central Bank Returns to Rate Cuts Path After Unrest Forced Hike
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

Turkey's central bank moved to lower borrowing costs for the first time in nearly five months, restarting an easing cycle that had been disrupted by a political furor.

The central bank said Thursday it would lower its benchmark rate by three points to 43% from 46%, marking the first time since early March that it has cut rates. Later that month, a prominent political rival to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan was arrested by police on what the opposition described as politically motivated charges. That move and the following protests, which were met with further arrests, spooked investors, leading to a series of swings in the Turkish lira.

The central bank subsequently in April raised its interest rates, reversing a course of rate cuts begun at the end of last year as inflation began to ease from the heights it had reached over the last few years. Rampant price inflation had hamstrung the lira and left the population battling ever-higher costs of living. Annual inflation touched a peak of more than 75% last year, but has cooled to around half that level as of June.

"Recent data indicate that the disinflationary impact of demand conditions has strengthened," the bank said in a statement.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: PRESSR: LSEG launches workspace add-in for Excel and PowerPoint
data.description:
LSEG Data & Analytics has launched a new Workspace Add-in for Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, now available to Workspace users with a Microsoft 365 licence. This marks a significant milestone in transforming the end-user experience and advancing the LSEG–Microsoft strategic partnership.

The new add-in brings enhanced data discoverability and usability directly into Excel and PowerPoint, allowing users to access LSEG’s trusted data more intuitively. Features include simplified formulas, enriched visualisations through Annotations and LSEG Charting tools, and reusable content via the Asset Library.

Nej D’Jelal, Head of Workspace and Interim Co-head of Workflows at LSEG, said:

“This is a significant milestone. We’re transforming how financial professionals discover and use data directly within Excel and PowerPoint. By enhancing workflows, we’re helping users make faster, smarter, and more confident decisions.”

Built for financial professionals, the add-in enables the creation of dynamic pitch books. Annotations streamline the discovery of news and events by enabling dynamic chart labelling, while rich metadata and custom tagging power seamless search and content sharing across Microsoft 365.

To learn more, click here

Media Contact

Elizabeth Manets / Tarek Fleihan

Communications, LSEG

newsroom@lseg.com

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Pool Corp Sees 2025 EPS $10.80-EPS $11.30 >POOL
Mobileye raises annual revenue forecast on autonomous driving chip demand
data.description:
Self-driving technology firm Mobileye Global raised its forecast for fiscal 2025 revenue on Thursday, anticipating a rise in orders for its autonomous driving chips as customers clear existing inventory.

Shares of the company rose more than 8% in premarket trading.

Automakers have largely resumed placing orders for self-driving hardware after an inventory surplus — caused by COVID-19-related supply concerns several years ago — led to a prolonged slump in demand.

Mobileye now expects annual revenue between $1.77 billion and $1.89 billion, compared with its previous forecast of $1.69 billion to $1.81 billion.

"Stronger visibility on industry supply-demand alignment since late-April supports our decision to raise the full-year outlook, while we continue to maintain a conservative stance given the broader macro environment," said Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua.

data.title: CenterPoint Energy misses profit estimate on higher costs
data.description:
U.S. electric and gas utility CenterPoint Energy's second-quarter profit was just shy of analysts' estimate on Thursday, weighed down by higher expenses.

The country's electrical grids have been facing an onslaught of extreme weather and ballooning demand from electrification of industries and the technology sector's data center build out amid an artificial intelligence boom, prompting utilities to seek rate increases to upgrade and pay for power infrastructure.

CenterPoint's operations and maintenance costs increased 5.5% to $715 million during the quarter ended June 30.

The company provides electricity and natural gas to more than 7 million customers across Indiana, Louisiana, Minnesota, Mississippi, Ohio and Texas.

It also raised its 10-year capital expenditure plan through 2030 by $500 million to $53 billion to cater to an anticipated surge in power demand from data centers.

"This year alone we have increased our capital investment plan by $5.5 billion, including the $500 million increase," said CEO Jason Wells.

Power demand from U.S. data centers is expected to nearly triple in the next three years and consume as much as 12% of the total electricity produced, according to a study by Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.

CenterPoint's net income fell about 13% to $198 million.

It posted an adjusted profit per share of 29 cents during the quarter, compared with analysts' average estimate of 30 cents, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Mobileye raises annual revenue forecast
data.description:
Self-driving technology firm Mobileye Global raised its forecast for fiscal 2025 revenue on Thursday, anticipating a rise in orders for its autonomous driving chips as customers clear existing inventory.

The company now expects annual revenue between $1.77 billion and $1.89 billion, compared with its previous forecast of $1.69 billion to $1.81 billion.

data.title: Dow cuts high dividend in half in reaction to 'lower-for-longer' earnings
data.description:
By Tomi Kilgore

Chemical company's dividend yield was the highest in the S&P 500, but remains relatively high after the cut

Shares of Dow Inc. sank in early Thursday trading, after the chemicals and plastics company chopped its dividend payout in half to save money, as the company struggles to deal with a prolonged downturn in earnings.

The company also reported a much wider-than-expected second-quarter loss, and revenue that declined more than expected, amid weakness in all of its business segments, particularly in packaging and specialty plastics.

The stock (DOW) dropped 7.7% in premarket trading. That puts it in danger of suffering the biggest one-day post-earnings selloff in at least five years, based on available FactSet data back to July 2020.

The company said the next dividend payment will be 35 cents a share, down from the latest payment of 70 cents a share. Shareholders of record on Aug. 29 will receive the dividend payment on Sept. 12.

"Today's announcement aims to ensure we maximize long-term shareholder value as we navigate a prolonged industry downturn and the resulting lower-for-longer earnings environment," said Chief Executive Jim Fitterling. "With this adjustment, we are aligning the payout size to provide additional financial flexibility."

As of Wednesday's close, Dow's stock carried a dividend yield of 9.22%, which made it the S&P 500 index's SPX highest-yielding component.

With the new annual dividend rate, and based on Wednesday's stock closing price of $30.37, the dividend yield would drop to 4.61%, which would make it the 30th-highest yielder in the S&P 500, and is still nearly four-times higher than the implied yield for the S&P 500 of 1.22%.

The dividend cut comes after Dow said earlier in July that it would lay off 800 employees, in addition to the 1,500 job cuts announced in January, in response to continued weak demand. At that time, the company also said it would close three plants in Europe due to weakness in the region.

"This quarter the Dow team advanced several aggressive actions in response to the lower-for-longer earnings environment that our industry is facing, amplified by recent trade and tariff uncertainties," Fitterling said.

He added the actions were needed as the company was providing "near-term cash support and earnings growth levers," which he expects to total more than $6 billion by next year.

As Fitterling explained, one of the big problems Dow was facing was oversupply from newer market entrants who are exporting to various regions at "anti-competitive" prices, which he believes requires "additional regulatory action to restore competitive dynamics." Sounds like he's pushing for higher tariffs.

Separately, the company said it swung to a second-quarter net loss of $835 million from net income of $439 million in the same period a year ago, due to restructuring and severance costs. Excluding those nonrecurring costs, the company still reported an adjusted per-share loss of 42 cents, compared with last year's earnings of 68 cents a share. The average analyst estimate compiled by FactSet was for a per-share loss of 17 cents.

Net sales fell 7.4% to $10.10 billion, below the FactSet consensus of $10.24 billion.

Among Dow's business segments, packaging and specialty plastics sales were down 8.9% to $5.03 billion, as volume increased 1% buy local prices sank 10% due to lower polymer pricing.

Industrial intermediates and infrastructure sales declined 5.6% to $2.79 billion, as volume fell 2% and prices were down 5%, and performance materials and coatings sales slipped 5.1% to $2.13 billion, as both volume and prices dropped 3%.

The stock has tumbled 24.3% in 2025 through Wednesday, while the S&P 500 has gained 8.1%.

-Tomi Kilgore

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

American Airlines forecasts bigger Q3 loss as sluggish demand hits fares
data.description:
American Airlines forecast a bigger-than-expected third-quarter loss on Thursday, hurt by sluggish domestic travel demand that is resulting in more unsold seats and an erosion in fares.

The U.S. carrier expects adjusted loss per share in the current quarter to be in the range of 10 cents to 60 cents, compared with analysts' estimates of a 7 cents loss, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Auto tech provider Garrett Q2 net sales up, raises FY forecast
data.description:
Overview

  • Auto tech provider Garrett Q2 2025 net sales rise 3% on favorable foreign currency impact

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q2 misses consensus, driven by unfavorable product mix

  • Co raises full-year 2025 outlook, reflecting favorable foreign currency impacts

Outlook

  • Garrett Motion raises 2025 net sales outlook to $3.4 bln-$3.6 bln

  • Company expects 2025 adjusted EBITDA of $590 mln-$650 mln

  • Garrett sees 2025 net income between $233 mln and $278 mln

  • Company anticipates 2025 adjusted free cash flow of $330 mln-$410 mln

Result Drivers

  • FOREIGN CURRENCY IMPACT - Net sales increase driven by favorable foreign currency translation and import tariff recoveries

  • PRODUCT MIX CHALLENGE - Unfavorable product mix due to weaker demand for aftermarket sales and diesel products

  • PROGRAM EXTENSIONS - Secured over $1 bln in light vehicle turbo program extensions, reinforcing leadership in turbocharging
